name: Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop
name_original: Villanueva de Campeán desde Peñausende – circular por Mayalde – Vía Verde de la Plata
description: This moderate 25.9-mile touring bicycle loop near Peñausende follows parts of the historic Vía de la Plata, offering varied terrain and scen
region_intro: Touring cycling around Peñausende offers varied landscapes, from rural scenery to historical routes. The region, situated in Zamora, Spain, features diverse terrain with moderate elevation changes. Cyclists can explore paths near the Duero River and sections of the historic Vía de la Plata. These characteristics provide a range of touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Peñausende The most popular touring cycling route is Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows sections of the historic Vía de la Plata through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puente de Sogo loop from Pereruela, a moderate 22.9 miles (36.8 km) path. This route features varied elevation changes and scenic rural views. Local touring cyclists also love the Zamora Stone Bridge – Monoliths of the Three Causeways loop from Pereruela, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail leading through historical areas and rural scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Peñausende is defined by rural scenery, historical routes like the Vía de la Plata, and paths near the Duero River. The network provides routes with varied elevation changes and lengths. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñausende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Peñausende?
A: The region around Peñausende offers diverse terrain, ranging from picturesque rural landscapes to routes with varied elevation changes. You'll find paths near the Duero River and sections of the historic Vía de la Plata, providing a mix of challenging climbs and more moderate stretches.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Peñausende?
A: While all listed routes are moderate, their varied landscapes and historical points of interest can be enjoyable for families with some cycling experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036951048/penausende-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to explore historical paths through rural scenery, which can be a rewarding experience for older children.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while cycling near Peñausende?
A: Peñausende is rich in history. Many routes follow parts of the ancient Vía de la Plata. You can also visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10412049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Stone Bridge – Monoliths of the Three Causeways loop from Pereruela&lt;/a&gt; also leads through historical areas.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy tranquil rural scenery and routes that follow the Duero River, offering scenic views. The wider Zamora province also features areas around reservoirs like Ricobayo and Valparaíso, and even glacial landscapes further afield, providing diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Peñausende are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036951048/penausende-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Sogo loop from Pereruela&lt;/a&gt; are both designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Peñausende?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a cycling route?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, Peñausende and nearby towns like Pereruela, which serve as starting points for routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10412049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Stone Bridge – Monoliths of the Three Causeways loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically offer public parking options in or near their town centers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: Many rural and natural trails in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific areas or natural parks you plan to enter. Ensure your dog is fit for the distance and terrain, and carry enough water for both of you.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay overnight near the cycling routes?
A: Peñausende and the surrounding villages offer local cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel. For accommodation, you'll find options ranging from guesthouses to rural hotels in these towns, providing convenient places to stay for cyclists exploring the region.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region's varied terrain and rural landscapes offer numerous scenic spots. Specific viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4420899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Salamanca Plains&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3019261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Cañada Real de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive vistas of the surrounding countryside.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The moderate touring cycling routes around Peñausende vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036951048/penausende-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 25.9 miles (41.7 km) and takes approximately 2 hours 56 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora – Zamora Stone Bridge loop from Villanueva de Campeán&lt;/a&gt; is 43.2 km and takes around 3 hours 28 minutes.

Q: Can I access Peñausende and the cycling routes using public transport with my bicycle?
A: Public transport options, such as buses, connect Peñausende with larger towns like Zamora. While some bus services may allow bicycles, it's crucial to check with the specific bus company beforehand regarding their policies, space availability, and any additional fees for bicycle transport.

The track is in very good condition and the views it gives you of the plain are impressive. From this point you can see the mosaic of colors offered by the fields of Salamanca from kilometers away.

The town of Peñausende is located in the Sayago region. On a rocky hill there are remains of an ancient settlement, where the old fortified castle stands, dominating the surrounding landscape and the commercial route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop?
faqs.answer: The Vía de la Plata, and by extension this loop, offers a rewarding experience in various seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. While the route is accessible year-round, be prepared for potentially hot summers and cooler, possibly wetter, winters. The open plains can be exposed to the elements, so checking the weather forecast is always a good idea.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a varied cycling experience. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including gravel, asphalt, concrete, and dirt sections. While much of the route uses quiet roads, there are also off-road segments. Some unpaved sections may require good fitness or even a short walk, making it ideal for touring, hybrid, or gravel bikes. Expect characteristic plains with cereal fields and rolling hills.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or landmarks along the Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You can visit the historic Convent of Nuestra Señora del Soto, enjoy expansive vistas from the View of the Salamanca Plains, and explore the ancient Castillo de Peñausende. The area is rich in historical remains from various periods, adding to the cultural heritage of your ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact loop aren't provided, Peñausende is the natural starting point. You should be able to find parking within the town itself, likely near the main square or local amenities, providing convenient access to the route.

faqs.question: Is the Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is graded as 'moderate' and generally accessible due to its mostly gentle climbs and empty roads. However, it's not entirely flat, featuring rolling hills, and some unpaved sections may require good fitness. If you're a beginner, ensure you have a suitable touring, hybrid, or gravel bike and are comfortable with varied surfaces and some elevation changes.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, and various segments of the Cañada Real de Vizana o de la Plata, which is a historic drovers' road.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on public trails in Spain, especially on rural routes like those found along the Vía de la Plata. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or on shared paths, to ensure safety and respect for local regulations and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Peñausende - Vía de la Plata Loop. The route primarily uses public roads and established trails, including sections of the historic Vía de la Plata and Vías Verdes, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: What makes this specific loop notable for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a blend of historical exploration, varied cycling terrain, and scenic views typical of the Castilian plains. It allows you to experience the rich cultural heritage of the Vía de la Plata, a route with Roman origins, while enjoying a mix of quiet roads and off-road sections, including former railway lines converted into easy cycle paths (Vías Verdes).

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: While the route is generally moderate, some unpaved sections can be challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike if you're not on a gravel or mountain bike. An insider tip is to be prepared for the open, vast landscapes; they are beautiful but can be exposed to sun and wind. Carrying enough water and snacks is advisable, as services might be sparse between small villages.
